page: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the n2 family. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon cores running at 2.8 GHz, with 2 threads per core. The memory configuration provides 8.0 GB of RAM, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU. The processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance.

Benchmark data positions the n2-highcpu-8 primarily in the average tier for general compute tasks, though it exhibits specific bottlenecks. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU scores, and Geekbench single-core metrics all fall within the middle 50% average category. However, multi-threaded decompression and compression tasks, such as bzip3 and gzip, register in the bottom 25% weak category. Similarly, Geekbench multi-core performance, ray tracing, and image processing are weak, reflecting the constraints of the 8-vCPU limit under heavy parallel workloads.

Memory performance is mixed; while small-block cached read bandwidth is average at 412.9 GB/sec, the overall Passmark memory score is weak. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for compute-heavy workloads that do not require large memory footprints, avoiding the overhead of unneeded RAM. It is suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations, but is less optimal for memory-intensive applications or heavy multi-threaded data processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 4 physical cores. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for single-core stress tests, software compilation, and static web serving, while multi-core operations, image processing, and memory-intensive tasks fall into the lower 25th percentile. With a low memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per vCPU, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for compute-bound workloads that do not demand large memory capacities, such as lightweight database operations, text processing, and small-scale web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, delivering average single-core performance for compute-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-highcpu-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, optimized for compute-heavy, low-memory workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and a low memory-to-core ratio for specialized processing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
8.0 GB of RAM providing a 1:1 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Average performance in software compilation and static web serving
Weak performance in memory-intensive and highly parallel multi-core tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for lightweight databases, text processing, and web serving
